author: aonosota
description: Extract selections or headings into new notes using multiple configurable profiles (template, folder, filename rule, link style). - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: aonosota
manifest.description: Extract selections or headings into new notes using multiple configurable profiles (template, folder, filename rule, link style).
manifest.name: Note Refactor Plus

name: Note Refactor Plus

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Bump to v0.1.5: migrate settings tab to declarative Settings API

Obsidian's automated plugin scorecard flagged 9 uses of the display()
override, deprecated since 1.13.0 in favor of getSettingDefinitions().
Rewrite NrpSettingsTab to the declarative API: global toggle/slider
bind directly to plugin settings keys, and the profile list uses the
framework's built-in reorder/delete affordances instead of a hand
rolled drag-and-drop implementation.

Also raise eslint.config.mjs to type-checked rules (matching what the
scorecard's other 12 warnings turned out to run) and fix the handful
of real lint findings that surfaced under it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/aonosota/note-refactor-plus/compare/0.1.4...0.1.5

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Bump to v0.1.4: fix code review bugs

Fixes 8 real bugs found in a full code review, including 3 that could
cause user-visible data loss or silent failures:

- pickFile() always rejected even when a file was selected, breaking
  append-existing profiles with a runtime file picker
- Bulk heading split could corrupt newly created notes when
  afterExtract opened a note mid-loop and swapped the shared editor's
  document
- Editing a profile didn't take effect until Obsidian was restarted,
  because per-profile commands closed over a stale profile reference
- Filename prompt cancellation surfaced as a misleading "Extract
  failed" error notice instead of aborting silently
- append-existing profiles could leave behind an unused destination
  folder
- The conflict modal's "Append to existing" choice hung forever if
  the target file was deleted while the modal was open
- splitFromCursor used a stale line-count snapshot across awaits and
  could pass an empty string as the filename source

Also addresses 5 community-plugin-review guideline items: Setting-
based section headings, CSS-class-based show/hide toggles instead of
inline styles, a stale doc comment, a placeholder string that could
be saved as a real filename pattern, and pinning the obsidian type
package version instead of "latest".

All fixes verified end-to-end in a live Obsidian test vault via the
Obsidian CLI (pickFile, bulk split, profile-edit reflection, filename
prompt cancel, and conflict-modal race were each reproduced and
confirmed fixed) in addition to passing tsc + esbuild.

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/aonosota/note-refactor-plus/compare/0.1.3...0.1.4

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Fix community plugin review: API compatibility, lint warnings, and bump to v0.1.3

- Bump minAppVersion to 1.13.1 to match installed obsidian package and clear no-unsupported-api errors
- Replace builtin-modules npm package with node:module builtinModules
- Fix Promise-in-void-context warnings: ProfileSuggester callback type, settings-tab event handlers, main.ts onunload
- Use app.vault.configDir instead of hardcoded .obsidian path
- Replace TFile cast with instanceof guard in conflict.ts
- Use window.setTimeout for popout window compatibility
- Use fileManager.trashFile() instead of vault.delete() to respect user preference
- Remove unnecessary TFile cast in context-menu.ts
- Remove deprecated setDynamicTooltip() call

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/aonosota/note-refactor-plus/compare/0.1.2...0.1.3

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Fix extracted content lost when template has no {{content}} placeholder

applyTemplate now appends the extracted text after the template body when the
template file contains no {{content}} variable, so frontmatter-only templates
(e.g. date/tags/aliases) no longer silently discard the selection.

Also shows a Notice when saving a profile whose template lacks {{content}},
so users are aware of the auto-append behaviour.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/aonosota/note-refactor-plus/compare/0.1.1...0.1.2

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Fix Extract Selection losing content when selection is cleared after async operations

Use editor.replaceRange() with positions captured before any awaits instead of
editor.replaceSelection(), which could operate on a stale/lost selection—most
noticeable with long text or when a profile picker modal ran before extraction.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/aonosota/note-refactor-plus/compare/0.1.0...0.1.1
